high_concern_flags.topic: Day and Temporary Labor Services Act 90-day equal-pay trigger
high_concern_flags.detail: Page references the Day and Temporary Labor Services Act 820 ILCS 175 equal-pay-plus-benefits trigger after 90 days at a host company in the cost-of-getting-it-wrong section. The 90-day trigger follows from HB 2862 (2023) and the SB 3650 (2024) implementing revisions. Verify the current comparator-employee equal-pay rule and benefit-equivalency methodology against the most recent Illinois Department of Labor rulemaking before relying on a specific compensation calculation for a client engagement.

high_concern_flags.topic: Construction Employee Classification Act civil-penalty figure
high_concern_flags.detail: Page cites the $1,500 per worker per day civil penalty under Employee Classification Act 820 ILCS 185/40, with doubling for second offence within five years and three-year state-contract debarment under 820 ILCS 185/45. Both figures follow primary statute and Illinois Department of Labor enforcement bulletins. Verify the exact penalty schedule against the current statute compilation before any client-pricing decision.

high_concern_flags.topic: Compliance cache prompt corrections inherited from sibling state-income-tax page
high_concern_flags.detail: Compliance cache for Illinois already records the 2026 SUI wage base correction ($14,250, not $13,916) and the new-employer rate correction (3.35% default, 3.45% NAICS 56, not the ~3.95% in older prompt guidance). This worker-classification page uses the corrected figures throughout the cost-of-misclassification table and the vs-other-states comparison section.

Page cites the August 2024 Cothron amendment (Public Act 103-0769) that limits BIPA damages to per-person rather than per-scan counting. Active class-action litigation continues to test the amendment's scope and retroactive application. Monitor 2026 Illinois Supreme Court rulings before relying on the per-person framing for any specific exposure calculation.

spec_notes: Modelled on Idaho sibling structure (most recent V2 mixed-test page) with Illinois-specific statute swaps throughout.

Three classification regimes distinguished in capsule + which-test section: IRS common-law for non-construction wage and hour; state ABC for UI under 820 ILCS 405/212; strict ABC for construction under 820 ILCS 185.
Freelance Worker Protection Act (820 ILCS 185.5; effective 1 July 2024) treated as a distinctive Illinois angle — written-contract rule for $500+ hires from a single hirer over any 120-day period.
BIPA (740 ILCS 14) covered as a contractor-relevant exposure because biometric authentication is common in fintech, healthcare, and warehousing workflows.

Personal liability for owners and officers under Wage Payment and Collection Act 820 ILCS 115/13 referenced in capsule and cost table — this is one of Illinois's most distinctive features vs other midwest common-law states.
Section 530 federal safe harbour treatment distinguished from state-side exposure.

Three named Illinois scenarios per task brief: Aaliyah (Chicago fintech dev, freelance; appears across multiple sections); Ezra (Springfield construction GC, ABC trigger); Priya (Naperville data analyst, borderline single-client 1099).
Capsule = 4-paragraph stake/size/ack/promise. Body prose statute-free per V2; statutes confined to Source columns of data tables and the end-of-page disclaimer.
End-of-page disclaimer uses task-brief exact specification.

stage_provenance.research: Used compliance cache illinois.json plus task-brief provisional facts list: construction ABC under 820 ILCS 185; common-law for non-construction; Day and Temporary Labor Services Act 820 ILCS 175 with HB 2862 (2023) and SB 3650 (2024) revisions; Wage Payment and Collection Act 820 ILCS 115 personal liability; Freelance Worker Protection Act 820 ILCS 185.5 (eff 1 Jul 2024); BIPA 740 ILCS 14; coordinated enforcement across IDOL + IDES + IDR; penalty schedule under 820 ILCS 185/40 ($1,500/day/worker for construction). Cross-checked against Idaho sibling (most recent V2 mixed-test page) and California sibling (pure ABC contrast).
